screens.name: Supervisor workflow contract
screens.purpose: Help agents execute deterministic workflow steps, preserve design evidence, and reopen refinements without ambiguity.
screens.primary_action: Run the next approved workflow step

ux_rules: Prefer deterministic wrapper actions for repeated workflow tasks instead of one-off shell command strings.
Require screenshot evidence for implementation-facing design review so critiques reference the rendered surface, not just code intent.
Keep design constraints structured so aesthetic expectations can be consumed mechanically by builder and critic stages.
Make refinement loops explicit so a completed run can re-enter work with a named reason and tracked revision.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Represent workflow expectations as structured protocol fields and deterministic actions rather than prose-only guidance.
interaction_risks: Design review may stay subjective if screenshot evidence remains optional.
Agents may bypass the intended workflow if common steps are still easier to run manually.
A run may falsely remain done if refinement is not modeled directly in run state.
notes: This run targets internal workflow infrastructure rather than a user-facing page.

constraints: Keep all changes additive and compatible with the current MVP supervisor architecture.
Use structured schema fields for new requirements instead of relying on more prose alone.
Prefer simple shell wrappers in actions/ for deterministic repeated tasks.
open_decisions: Whether action wrappers should later become Node scripts instead of shell scripts remains open.
Whether screenshot evidence should live in design-review only or also be mirrored in verify remains open.

critical_judgments: The new wrappers should reduce manual repetition without hiding what commands they run.
Schema additions should be explicit enough to improve quality without overcomplicating the MVP.
Refinement tracking should reopen work clearly and predictably.
must_not_regress: Repository rule that code edits go through supervisor

visual_constraints: Favor explicit contract fields over interpretive prose when a workflow expectation needs to be enforced repeatedly.
Prefer lightweight wrappers and metadata over heavy orchestration for this iteration.
Keep documentation terse and operational rather than aspirational.
